pattern: Monotonic stack that finds the next smaller-or-equal element for each price — the classic 'next smaller element' pattern applied left to right.
hints: For each item i, the discount is prices[j] where j is the smallest index > i with prices[j] <= prices[i]; this is a 'next smaller or equal' query.
Use a stack of indices in decreasing price order: when a new price is less than or equal to the stack top, pop and apply it as the discount.
Items remaining on the stack after the scan received no discount — their final price equals their original price.

edge_inputs.description: Strictly decreasing — every item except last gets discounted by next
Statement example 3 — equal prices trigger discount
Equal adjacent prices — discount applies (<=, not just <)
Discount reduces to zero — price[j] == price[i]
